title: Pesticidally-active bicyclic heteroaromatic compounds
abstract: A compound of formula (I) wherein the substituents are as defined in claim 1, and the agrochemically acceptable salts, stereoisomers, enantiomers, tautomers and N-oxides of those compounds, can be used as insecticides.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . A compound of Formula (I): wherein: W is O or S; R 1 is Ci-C6alkyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2, or Ci-C6haloalkyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2; or C2-C6alkenyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2, or C2-C6haloalkenyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2; or C2-C6alkynyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2, or C2-C6haloalkynyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2 or C3-C6cycloalkyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents independently selected from U3 or a single substituent selected from U2; or cyano, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonyl or phenylcarbonyl; Ui is nitro, cyano, amino, hydroxyl, CO2H, C3-C6cycloalkyl, C3-C6halocycloalkyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxy, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy, Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkoxy, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl, Ci- C 4 alkoxycarbonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxycarbonyl, C3-C 4 cycloalkylcarbonyl, =N- 0(Ci-C 4 alkyl), =N-0(Ci-C 4 haloalkyl), -Si(Ci-C 4 alkyl) 3 , (Ci-C 4 haloalkyl)NH-, (Ci-C 4 haloalkyl) 2 N-, (C1-C4 haloalkyl)(Ci-C 4 alkyl)N-, (Ci-C 4 alkyl)NH-, (Ci-C 4 alkyl) 2 N-, (C3-C 6 cycloalkyl)NH-, (C3-C 6 cycloalkyl)(Ci- C 4 alkyl)N-, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonylamino, Ci-C 4 haloalkylcarbonylamino, aminocarbonyl, N-Ci- C 4 alkylaminocarbonyl, (N,N-di-Ci-C 4 alkylamino)carbonyl, C3-C6cycloalkylaminocarbonyl, Ci- C 4 haloalkylaminocarbonyl, C3-C6halocycloalkylaminocarbonyl or phenylaminocarbonyl, wherein the 

 phenyl group is optionally substitued by a single substituent selected from halogen, methyl, methoxy or trifluoromethyl; U2 is phenyl, heteroaryl wherein the heteroaryl moiety is a 5- or 6-membered monocyclic aromatic ring which comprises 1 , 2, 3, or 4 heteroatoms individually selected from nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur, or a 5- or 6-membered saturated or partially saturated heterocyclic ring wherein the heterocyclic ring comprises 1 to 3 heteroatoms selected from nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur, and wherein each phenyl, heteroaryl or heterocyclic ring is optionally substituted by: (i) 1 , 2, 3, 4 or 5 independently selected halogen groups, or (ii) 1 or 2 groups independently selected from L4 and optionally a halogen group; L is halogen, nitro, cyano, amino, hydroxyl, -CO2H, Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkyl, C3-C6cycloalkyl, C3-C6halocycloalkyl, C3-C6halocycloalkyl-Ci-C 4 alkyl, C3-C6cycloalkyl-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxy, Ci- C 4 alkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkyl, cyano-Ci-C 4 alkyl, cyano-Ci- C 4 haloalkyl, C2-C6alkenyl, C2-C6haloalkenyl, C2-C6alkynyl, C2-C6haloalkynyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfanyl, Ci- C 4 alkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxycarbonyl and - C(0)NH 2 ; U4 is nitro, cyano, amino, hydroxyl, -SCN, -CO2H, Ci-C 4 alkyl, C3-C6cycloalkyl, C3- Cehalocycloalkyl, C3-C6cycloalkyl-Ci-C 4 alkyl, C3-C6halocycloalkyl-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkyl, Ci- C 4 alkoxy, Ci-C 4 alkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkoxy, cyano-Ci-C 4 alkyl, cyano-Ci-C 4 haloalkyl, C 2 -C 4 alkenyl, C 2 -C 4 haloalkenyl, C 2 -C 4 alkynyl, C 2 -C 4 haloalkynyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy-Ci- C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfanyl, Ci- C 4 haloalkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonyl, Ci- C 4 haloalkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxycarbonyl, (Ci-C 4 alkyl)NH-, (Ci-C 4 alkyl) 2 N-, (C3-C6cycloalkyl)NH-, (C3-C6cycloalkyl) 2 N-, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonylamino, C3-C6cycloalkylcarbonylamino, Ci- C 4 haloalkylcarbonylamino, C3-C6halocycloalkylcarbonylamino, Ci-C 4 alkylaminocarbonyl, C3-C6 cycloalkylaminocarbonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylaminocarbonyl, C3-C6halocycloalkylaminocarbonyl, C3-C6 cycloalkylcarbonyl, C3-C6 halocycloalkylcarbonyl, -SFs or -C(0)NH2; m is 0, 1 or 2; R 2 is independently selected from halogen, cyano, amino, hydroxyl, Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy, Ci-C 4 alkoxy, C 2 -C 4 alkenyl, C 2 -C 4 haloalkenyl, C 2 -C 4 alkynyl, C 2 -C 4 haloalkynyl, C1-C4 alkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfinyl, Ci- C 4 haloalkylsulfonyl and cyclopropyl; R 3a and R 3b are independently selected from hydrogen, halogen, Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkyl, Ci- C 4 alkoxy and cyano; 

 R 4 is selected from one of Y1 to Y4: wherein n is 0, 1 or 2; and U is independently selected from halogen, cyano, nitro, hydroxyl, amino, Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci- C 4 haloalkyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxy, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy, C 2 -C 4 alkenyl, C 2 -C 4 haloalkenyl, C 2 -C 4 alkynyl, C 2 -C 4 haloalkynyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfanyl, Ci- C 4 alkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 alkylsulfonyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfinyl, Ci-C 4 haloalkylsulfonyl, formyl, cyclopropyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl or C3-C6cycloalkylcarbonyl; or an agrochemically acceptable salt, stereoisomer, enantiomer, tautomer or N-oxide thereof. 2. A compound according to claim 1 , wherein R 1 is: Ci-C6alkyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2, or Ci-C 4 haloalkyl; or C2-C6alkenyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2, or C2-C6haloalkenyl, or C2-C6alkynyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents selected from Ui or a single substituent selected from U2, or C2-C6haloalkynyl, or C3-C6cycloalkyl optionally substituted by 1 or 2 substituents selected from U3; or Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl or phenylcarbonyl; and Ui is cyano, C3-C6cycloalkyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxy, C3-C 4 halocycloalkyl, Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkoxy, Ci- C 4 alkylsulfanyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonyl, C3-C 4 cycloalkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonyl, N-0(Ci-C 4 alkyl); U2 is phenyl, thienyl, thiazolyl, pyridinyl, pyrimidinyl or pyrazinyl, each optionally substituted by (i) 1 , 2 or 3 independently selected halogen groups, or (ii) a single L4 group selected from cyano and Ci-C 4 haloalkoxy; and U3 is independently selected from halogen, Ci-C 4 alkyl or Ci-C 4 haloalkyl. 3. A compound according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein R 1 is selected from Ci-C6alkyl, Ci- Cehaloalkyl, cyanoCi-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C 2 alkoxy-Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkyl, C2-C6alkenyl, C2-C6haloalkenyl, C 2 -C 4 alkynyl, C3-C6cycloalkyl, (C3-C6cycloalkyl)methyl, Ci- C 4 alkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonylCi-C3alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonylCi-C 2 alkyl, 

 Ci-C 2 alkylsulfanylCi-C 4 alkyl, cyclopropylC(0)methyl, (halocyclopropyl)methyl, -CH(C(0)0Ci-C 4 alkyl) 2 , (Ci-C 4 alkyl-0-N=)Ci-C 4 alkyl, (Ci-C 4 alkyl-0-N=)Ci-C 4 alkyl, phenylaminocarbonylCi-C 4 alkyl wherein the phenyl group is optionally substituted by 1 , 2 or 3 halogen atoms, or (phenyl)methyl, (thienyl)methyl, (pyridinyl)methyl, or (pyrazinyl)methyl, wherein each phenyl, thienyl, pyridinyl or pyrazinyl group is optionally substituted by a single group selected from chloro, fluoro, cyano, methyl, ethyl, ethoxy, difluoromethoxy or trifluoromethoxy. 4. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein R 1 is selected from Ci-C 4 alkyl, Ci- C6haloalkyl, cyanoCi-C 4 alkyl, Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkyl, Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkoxy-Ci-C2alkyl, C2-C6alkenyl, C 2 -C 4 fluoroalkenyl, C 2 -C 4 alkynyl, C3-C 4 cycloalkyl, (C3-C 4 cycloalkyl)methyl, Ci- C 4 alkylcarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonyl, Ci-C 4 alkoxycarbonylCi-C3alkyl, Ci-C 4 alkylcarbonylCi-C 2 alkyl, Ci-C 2 alkylsulfanylCi-C 4 alkyl, cyclopropyiC(0)methyl, (difluorocyclopropyl)methyl, -CH(C(0)0Ci- C 4 alkyl) 2 , (Ci-C 4 alkyl-0-N=)Ci-C 4 alkyl, (Ci-C 4 alkyl-0-N=)Ci-C 4 alkyl, phenylaminocarbonylCi-C 4 alkyl wherein the phenyl group is optionally substituted by 1 , 2 or 3 independently selected chloro or fluoro atoms, or (phenyl)methyl, (thien-2-yl)methyl, (pyridin-2-yl)methyl, or (pyrazinyl)methyl, wherein each phenyl, thienyl, pyridinyl or pyrazinyl group is optionally substituted by a single group selected from chloro, fluoro, cyano, methyl, ethyl, ethoxy, difluoromethoxy or trifluoromethoxy. 5. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein R 1 is selected from -CFhCECH, - CH 2 CF 2 H, cyclopropyl, -CF 2 H, -CH 2 CH 2 CN, -CH 2 CN, (2,2-difluorocyclopropyl)methyl, CH 2 C(0)cyclopropyl, -CF2CF2H, -CH2CF2H, -CH2CF3, -C(0)0tBu, -CH2CH2F, -CH2CH2CH2CF3, - CH2CH2OCH2OCH3, -CH2CH2CH2OCH2CH3, -CH(C0 2 CH 3 )2, (5-cyanothien-2-yl)methyl, trifluoromethoxyphenyl)methyl, (3-difluoromethoxyphenyl)methyl, (cyclobutyl)methyl, -CH 2 CH 2 CI, (5- fluoropyridin-2-yl)methyl, (pyrazinyl)methyl, -CH2CH2CH=CH2, -CH2CH2CH2CH=CH2, CH(CH 3 )C(H)=NOCH 2 CH3, -CH 2 C(CH 3 )=NOCH3, -CH(CH3)C(0)NH(3-CF 3 Ph), -CH2CH2CH2CI, - CH2CH3, or -CH2C(0)cyclopropyl. 6. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein m is 0 or 1 , and preferably 0. 7. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein R 3a is hydrogen and R 3b is hydrogen. 8. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein R 4 is selected from Y2 or Y3. 9. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein n is 1. 10. A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein R 4 is: 1 1 . A compound according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein R 4 is: 12. An agrochemical composition comprising an insecticidally, acaricidally, nematicidally or molluscicidally effective amount of a compound according to any one of claims 1 to 1 1 . 13. The composition according to claim 12, further comprising at least one additional active ingredient and/or an agrochemically-acceptable diluent or carrier. 14. A method of controlling insects, acarines, nematodes or molluscs which comprises applying an insecticidally, acaricidally, nematicidally or molluscicidally effective amount of a compound of formula (I) as defined in any one of claims 1 to 1 1 , or a composition comprising this compound as active ingredient, to a pest, a locus of pest (preferably a plant), to a plant susceptible to attack by a pest or to plant a propagation material thereof (such as a seed). 15. Use of a compound according to any one of claims 1 to 1 1 as an insecticide, acaricide, nematicide or molluscicide.
